def magicalString(self, n: int) -> int:

efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(3)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
We iterate through the string from the second character to the n-1 character. For each character, we update the count of the corresponding digit (1 or 2) in a 3-element list. Finally, we return the sum of the counts of digits 1 and 2. This solution has a time complexity of O(n) since we iterate through the string once, and a space complexity of O(3) due to the small size of the count list. The energy implications are low since the solution only involves simple arithmetic operations and does not require any complex data structures or algorithms.
